message:
fix(raptorq): include constraint symbols in conformance tests + fix test assertions

The conformance test helper `build_received_symbols()` was missing the
S+H constraint symbols (LDPC + HDPC parity checks with zero RHS) that
the decoder requires. All internal unit tests correctly start with
`decoder.constraint_symbols()` — the conformance suite was the only
caller that skipped them, causing SingularMatrix failures.

Also fixes:
- insufficient_symbols_fails: source slice index used L-1 but source
  only has K elements (L > K due to S+H overhead)
- soliton_distribution_coverage: for small K (10), the robust soliton's
  tau boost at threshold makes degree 2 more probable than degree 1
- params_overhead_bounded: for K=10, overhead is 110% due to minimum
  S=7, H=3; use per-K bounds instead of a flat 50% threshold

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
